data.title: Fitt's Law
data.description: Interface design follows Fitt’s Law to ensure that target areas are sized appropriately for efficient user interaction.
data.technical_spec: User interface elements are designed with accessibility and ease-of-use in mind.

data.title: Hick's Law
data.description: Simplification of choices in the UI minimizes the time required for users to make decisions.
data.technical_spec: Implementing streamlined navigation and reducing cognitive load across platforms.

data.title: Miller's Law
data.description: Design elements are structured to present information in chunks, easing cognitive processing.
data.technical_spec: Information architecture utilizes grouping to support Miller's limit of human memory.

data.title: Jakob's Law
data.description: Ensuring consistency with user expectations by following familiar conventions in UI design.
data.technical_spec: Adopts design patterns that users are accustomed to from other industry-standard apps.

data.title: Law of Proximity
data.description: Elements within a UI are positioned in relation to each other to indicate association and improve user perception.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes proximity in layout design to enhance visual cohesion and usability.
